summary: Reference context covers the right specs (tuple-ops.md, builtins.md, class.md, value-and-rc.md, hir-to-mir.md, symbols.md) and source line numbers are largely accurate. However two factual errors in key requirements would directly mislead the implementer: (1) value-and-rc.md claims ObjData::Dict is IndexMap<u64,(MbValue,MbValue)> — the actual type in rc.rs:123 is RwLock<IndexMap<String,MbValue>>, a different key type and value shape; this matters for mb_type_new which must iterate the dict argument. (2) class.md claims MbClass has a slots: Option<Vec<String>> field — MbClass has no such field; __slots__ is managed via a separate SLOTS_REGISTRY thread_local. Additionally the spec plan is missing two required entries: symbols.md (explicitly called out in its own key requirements as needing mb_type_new registration) and class.md (high relevance, mb_type_new must invoke mb_class_register and mb_class_set_class_attr from class.rs).

findings.location: reference_context.md — value-and-rc.md key requirements: wrong Dict type
findings.description: Reference context states: 'ObjData::Dict uses IndexMap<u64, (MbValue, MbValue)>: for type() 3-arg, iterating the namespace dict to extract method names and values requires working with this IndexMap structure.' The actual ObjData::Dict in rc.rs line 123 is Dict(RwLock<IndexMap<String, super::value::MbValue>>) — keys are String (not u64), and values are MbValue (not (MbValue,MbValue) tuples). An implementer writing mb_type_new to iterate the dict argument based on this description would produce code that does not compile or behaves incorrectly. The spec in value-and-rc.md R3 also states this wrong type, so the reference context is reproducing a spec inaccuracy as if it were truth.

findings.location: reference_context.md — class.md key requirements: MbClass has no slots field
findings.description: Reference context states MbClass struct (line 18) has fields including 'slots: Option<Vec<String>>'. The actual MbClass struct (class.rs lines 18-30) has: name: String, bases: Vec<String>, mro: Vec<String>, methods: HashMap<String,MbValue>, class_attrs: HashMap<String,MbValue>, metaclass: Option<String>. There is NO slots field. __slots__ support is implemented via a separate SLOTS_REGISTRY thread_local (class.rs lines 41-43). While mb_type_new itself does not need to set slots, this inaccuracy would mislead anyone working with MbClass construction or extending the class system.

The reference context's own key requirements for symbols.md explicitly state: 'For #974: mb_type_new must be added to symbols.rs with signature [I64, I64, I64] -> I64 following the rt_sym! macro pattern.' Despite this, the spec plan has no entry to modify symbols.md. Without this entry, the implementation spec phase will have no documented requirement to add the rt_sym! registration for mb_type_new, and the function cannot be called from JIT-compiled code. This is a direct gap between the reference context body and the spec plan.

findings.location: spec_plan — class.md (high relevance) has no spec plan entry
findings.description: class.md is listed as 'high' relevance with substantive key requirements describing how mb_type_new will invoke mb_class_register (class.rs:48) and mb_class_set_class_attr (class.rs:198). These are interface points that should be captured in a spec plan modify entry for class.md. The new mb_type_new → mb_class_register integration path is a new usage of the class system API and should be spec-documented there, even if no new functions are added to class.rs itself.

findings.location: spec_plan — type-3arg-lowering-dispatch modifies hir-to-mir.md: no new R-ID specified
findings.description: The existing hir-to-mir.md spec already has R10/R11/R12 for issue #1133 (__name__ module initialization, a separate change). The spec plan adds sections 'overview, changes' for the type() 3-arg dispatch (#974), but does not define a new requirement ID. The implementer needs a clear R-ID (e.g., R13) to attach the new type() 3-arg special-case dispatch requirement to, and to avoid renaming conflicts with existing R10-R12.

findings.location: spec_plan — type-3arg-dynamic-class: no conformance test entry
findings.description: The spec plan for type-3arg-dynamic-class (builtins.md modification) lists sections 'overview, changes' but includes no test file additions. By contrast, the tuple-ordering-operators spec (already authored in tuple-ops.md) explicitly lists conformance test file modifications. For #974, at minimum a conformance test fixture (e.g., tests/fixtures/conformance/type_3arg.py) should be listed in the changes section to verify dynamic class creation behavior.

Source verification confirmed: mb_tuple_lt (line 279), mb_tuple_le (line 289), mb_tuple_gt (line 302), mb_tuple_ge (line 312) exist in tuple_ops.rs and are correctly described. seq_lt confirmed at builtins.rs line 887. Only mb_tuple_len (line 205) and mb_tuple_getitem (line 206) are in the rt_sym! table — mb_tuple_lt/le/gt/ge are NOT registered — confirmed accurate. The note that tuple comparison works via the generic mb_lt/le/gt/ge path (registered at symbols.rs lines 94, 111-113) is correct.

Source verification confirmed: mb_lt (line 852) calls mb_values_lt; mb_values_lt (line 857) dispatches to seq_lt for ObjData::Tuple (line 877); seq_lt at line 887 iterates element pairs with mb_values_lt recursively and uses a.len() < b.len() as tiebreaker. mb_gt (line 1851) = mb_lt(b,a); mb_le (line 1856) = mb_lt(a,b) || mb_eq(a,b); mb_ge (line 1865) = mb_le(b,a). mb_type at line 530 is 1-arg only and returns an Instance{class_name='type', __name__ field} via make_type_object. All line numbers and implementation descriptions are accurate.

Source verification confirmed: builtin_extern_map() at line 14 maps 'type' → 'mb_type'. Special-case dispatch pattern for mb_int_base at lines 3361-3370 and mb_getattr_default at lines 3410-3420 are confirmed accurate. The description of the pattern to follow for mb_type_new 3-arg dispatch is correct.

findings.location: reference_context.md — class.md key requirements: mb_class_register line number
findings.description: Source verification confirmed: mb_class_register is at class.rs line 48 with signature (name: &str, bases: Vec<String>, methods: HashMap<String,MbValue>). mb_class_set_class_attr is at line 198. CLASS_REGISTRY is a thread_local at lines 33-35. The description of using mb_class_register as the building block for type() 3-arg is architecturally correct.

Correctly rated medium. The observation that mb_tuple_lt/le/gt/ge exist in tuple_ops.rs but are not in the rt_sym! table (only mb_tuple_len/getitem at lines 205-206) is confirmed accurate. The note that mb_lt/le/gt/ge ARE registered (lines 94, 111-113) and dispatch through mb_values_lt is correct, meaning tuple comparison via generic path already works.

The observation that tuple_edge_cases.py tests < and <= and >= (confirmed: lines 10-12) and tuple_ops.py tests <, ==, != (confirmed: lines 9-13) and that > comparison is absent from all test files is accurate. tuple_ops.py has no > test; tuple_edge_cases.py also has none. This gap is correctly identified.

revisions_required: Fix value-and-rc.md key requirements: replace 'ObjData::Dict uses IndexMap<u64, (MbValue, MbValue)>' with the accurate type: Dict(RwLock<IndexMap<String, MbValue>>) — keys are String (the method name), values are MbValue (the callable). The mb_type_new implementation iterates `IndexMap<String, MbValue>` entries directly, not (MbValue,MbValue) tuples.

Fix class.md key requirements: remove the claim that MbClass has 'slots: Option<Vec<String>>'. The actual MbClass fields are: name, bases, mro, methods, class_attrs, metaclass. __slots__ is tracked in SLOTS_REGISTRY (class.rs line 41-43, a separate thread_local HashMap). Update the field listing to match the actual struct definition at class.rs lines 18-30.

Add a spec plan entry for symbols.md modification: { spec_id: 'type-3arg-symbol-registration', action: 'modify', main_spec_ref: 'crates/cclab-mamba/runtime/symbols.md', sections: ['requirements', 'changes'] } to capture the rt_sym! entry for mb_type_new with signature [I64, I64, I64] -> I64.

Add a spec plan entry for class.md modification (or at minimum clarify rationale for omitting it): if mb_type_new calls mb_class_register and mb_class_set_class_attr, the class.md spec should document this new integration path under a new requirement or an update to the existing class creation requirements.

Assign a specific new requirement ID (e.g., R13) for the type() 3-arg lowering dispatch in the hir-to-mir.md spec plan entry, to avoid collision with existing R10/R11/R12 which cover a separate issue (#1133).
